items.title: Match of the Day
items.summary: Highlights from five Premier League games, including Man City against Newcastle United.

items.title: Vink laakt houding Ajax-spelers: 'Ze staan er maar een beetje bij'
items.summary: NEC heeft zichzelf tekort gedaan door gelijk te spelen op bezoek bij Ajax ( 1-1 ). Dat was de conclusie van ESPN-analytici Arnold Bruggink en Marciano Vink.

items.title: Ronaldo bags brace as Al Nassr ease past Al Hazem
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Al Nassr beat Al Hazem 4-0 to move top of the Saudi Pro League.

items.title: City beat Newcastle, go within 2 points of Arsenal
items.summary: Nico O'Reilly scored twice in the first half as Manchester City cut Arsenal's Premier League lead to two points with a nervy 2-1 win over Newcastle.

items.title: Arbeloa: Madrid 'much better' than Osasuna loss
items.summary: Álvaro Arbeloa admitted Real Madrid "can play much better" after a 2-1 defeat at Osasuna on Saturday saw the team drop points in the LaLiga title race.

items.title: Rosenior: Chelsea 'set fire to 4 points' in 2 games
items.summary: Liam Rosenior said he is learning who he can and cannot rely on after Chelsea had Wesley Fofana sent off before throwing away two points in added time in a 1-1 draw against Burnley.

items.title: Fofana and Hannibal subjected to racist abuse after Chelsea-Burnley draw
items.summary: Chelsea centre-back Wesley Fofana highlighted some of the racist abuse he received on Instagram after the Blues' draw with Burnley, with Clarets midfielder Hannibal also subject to racist abuse.

items.title: Manchester City 2-1 Newcastle: Premier League – live reaction
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Follow us on Bluesky | And email Scott Newcastle get the ball rolling! “No-one actually believes we’ll win this game, do they?” sighs Toon fan Chris Paraskevas. “I mean the last time we won at the Etihad, the goal-scorers were Moussa Sissoko (now ruining his reputation at Panathinaikos with Rafa Benitez) and Ryan Taylor.” The teams are out. Manchester City, in sky blue, are given a guard of honour by members of their 1976 League Cup winning side. The 50th anniversary of that victory, over Newcastle, comes up next week. The Toon in third-choice blue. As for the weather, Bert Challenor, the talent scout from Comedians by Trevor Griffiths, says it best: “I’ll never understand why they don’t run boats to Manchester.” We’ll be off in a minute. Continue reading...

items.title: B-ploeg freewheelt PSG terug naar Franse koppositie tegen hekkensluiter Metz
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain heeft de koppositie in Frankrijk weer overgenomen van RC Lens. De Parijzenaars maakten zaterdagavond geen fout tegen hekkensluiter FC Metz en profiteerden zo van puntenverlies van de concurrent eerder op de dag.

items.title: VI Live: Feyenoord mogelijk lachende derde na gelijkspel in Arena
items.summary: Ajax neemt het zaterdag op tegen NEC. De twee ploegen zijn dit seizoen concurrenten van elkaar in de strijd om plek twee, goed voor een direct Champions League-ticket. Het duel gaat om 21.00 uur van start en volg je in deze VI Live.

items.title: Godts laat Ajax ontsnappen aan Nijmeegs pressieplan
items.summary: Ajax en NEC zijn in de strijd om plek twee geen stap verder gekomen. In de Johan Cruijff Arena eindigt een vermakelijk duel op gelijke hoogte (1-1). Een uitslag waar vooral de Nijmeegse uitdagers van zullen balen. Hoe Ajax worstelde met de hoge druk van NEC.

items.title: VI Live: Lookman floreert bij Atlético Madrid onder Simeone
items.summary: Zaterdagavond staat er met NAC Breda tegen FC Volendam een ware degradatiekraker op het programma. De duels van PSV en Ajax volgen we in een apart liveblog. Verder staat ons een druk programma in het buitenland te wachten. Mis niets met VI Live.

items.title: Motherwell romp to victory over St Mirren
items.summary: Motherwell treated the watching Sir Alex Ferguson to a football masterclass as Jens Berthel Askou’s side romped to a 5-0 win over St Mirren.

items.title: Alcaraz speeds to Qatar title as Pegula wins in Dubai
items.summary: Australian Open champion Carlos Alcaraz dispatched Frenchman Arthur Fils 6-2 6-1 in just 50 minutes to win the Qatar ExxonMobil Open in Doha.

items.title: West Ham miss chance to boost survival hopes with wasteful draw against Bournemouth
items.summary: When it comes to the back end of May and West Ham reflect on the season that was, there is every chance that this soggy Saturday will go down as a missed opportunity that proved decisive in their survival plight. Victory here would have raised the possibility of Nuno Espírito Santo’s side ending the weekend out of the relegation zone for the first time since early December. In keeping with those over recent weeks, much of the performance was befitting of another three points. But, thanks to a couple of smart Djordje Petrovic saves and some profligacy in front of goal, the hosts had to make do with a draw – an expected goals (xG) total of 2.87 from 20 shots yielding a blank in the only column that matters. Continue reading...

items.title: Red cards and poor home form - Chelsea 'set fire to points'
items.summary: Chelsea have more red cards than any side in the Premier League this season, and boss Liam Rosenior says they have "set fire" to points.

items.title: 'Like landing on the moon': How Milner set a near-unbreakable Premier League record
items.summary: At age 40, James Milner is now the Premier League's all-time appearances leader. Teammates and coaches -- from Ashley Young to Jurgen Klopp -- share what makes him special.

items.title: 'Like a dream' - Abraham relishing Villa return
items.summary: Seven years since firing them into the Premier League, Tammy Abraham is back scoring goals at Aston Villa to keep their fans and players dreaming of glory.

items.title: Scotland leave it late to edge Wales in Cardiff
items.summary: Scotland left it late to deny Wales a first Six Nations victory for three years, edging in front with five minutes to play to seal a 26-23 victory in Cardiff.

items.title: From £70 a week to Premier League record 24 years later
items.summary: James Milner has broken the Premier League appearance record, 24 years after making his debut as a 16-year-old.
